description: You are a data scientist analyzing the UCI Wine Quality dataset to identify natural groupings in wine chemical properties. Perform an unsupervised learning analysis pipeline using PCA and K-means clustering on the combined red and white wine datasets located at `/home/ga/RProjects/datasets/winequality-red.csv` and `/home/ga/RProjects/datasets/winequality-white.csv` (semicolon delimited). The analysis involves combining the datasets with a 'wine_type' column and conducting PCA on the 11 numeric chemical features (excluding quality and wine_type) using centered and scaled data. Export the PCA summary with columns for component, eigenvalue, proportion_variance, and cumulative_variance to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/wine_pca_summary.csv`. Conduct a silhouette analysis for K-means clustering with k ranging from 2 to 8 using the first five principal components, saving the average silhouette widths to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/wine_silhouette.csv`. Apply K-means clustering to the first five PCs using the optimal k identified from the silhouette analysis. Save the resulting observation-level data, including observation index, cluster, wine_type, quality, and PC1 through PC5, to `/home/ga/RProjects/output/wine_cluster_results.csv`. Generate a 4-panel diagnostic plot at `/home/ga/RProjects/output/wine_analysis.png` featuring a scree plot, a PCA biplot (PC1 vs PC2 colored by wine_type), a silhouette plot, and a cluster plot (PC1 vs PC2 colored by cluster). Write and execute the complete analysis script at `/home/ga/RProjects/wine_analysis.R` to generate all required outputs.
